prompt_text:
### Demographics: 66-year-old Asian woman

### Visit type: Follow-up visit (12-month follow-up)

### Reason for visit / current symptoms
Reason for visit: Routine follow-up (every 3 months) | Diagnosis context: 16 years ago Diagnosed with type 2 diabetes during urgent care evaluation for fatigue/blurred vision
Current symptoms: Rare nocturnal hypoglycemia (1 episode in 3 months); no DKA/HHS signs; Intermittent GI discomfort (nausea/bloating)

### History
- Other conditions: Chronic kidney disease Stage 4 (15-29)
Heart failure: HFrEF (NYHA Class II)
ASCVD: Ischemic stroke or transient ischemic attack history
Body mass index category: Normal
Diabetic retinopathy
Diabetic Retinopathy
Major Depressive Disorder
Osteoporosis (on bisphosphonate therapy)
Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease
- Diabetes-related comorbidities: CKD: Stage 4 (15-29); HF: HFrEF (NYHA Class II); ASCVD: Ischemic stroke or transient ischemic attack history; MASLD: Normal; BMI Category: Normal
- Family history: Type 2 diabetes (younger sister)
- Social history: Former smoker (quit 3 years ago; 20 cigarettes/day for 10 years; ~10 pack-years); Alcohol 5-6 drinks/week (1-2 per sitting)

### Medication information
- Current medications: Dulaglutide 1.5mg once weekly; Empagliflozin 10mg once daily; Sertraline (50 mg daily), Fluticasone/salmeterol Inhaler (twice daily), Rosuvastatin (10 mg daily), Losartan (50 mg daily), Alendronate (70 mg weekly), Calcium Carbonate 500 Mg + Cholecalciferol 1000 Iu (daily)

### Physical exam (current)
Weight 53.1 kg; Height 161.6 cm; BMI 20.3 kg/m²; Blood pressure 111/64 mmHg

### Laboratory values (with date / relative timing)
Format: YYYY-MM-DD value or relative timing (12 months ago / 9 months ago / 6 months ago / 3 months ago / current)
Order: 12 months ago -> 9 months ago -> 6 months ago -> 3 months ago -> current
- Glycemia: HbA1c (12 months ago 7.6%; 9 months ago 7.6%; 6 months ago 7.1%; 3 months ago 7.6%; current 5.8%), FPG (12 months ago 192mg/dL; 9 months ago 155mg/dL; 6 months ago 187mg/dL; 3 months ago 154mg/dL; current 108mg/dL)
- Anthropometrics / blood pressure: Weight (12 months ago 52.8kg; 9 months ago 53.3kg; 6 months ago 52.8kg; 3 months ago 50.3kg; current 53.1kg), Height (12 months ago 161.6cm; 9 months ago 161.6cm; 6 months ago 161.6cm; 3 months ago 161.6cm; current 161.6cm), BMI (12 months ago 20.0kg/m²; 9 months ago 20.0kg/m²; 6 months ago 20.0kg/m²; 3 months ago 19.0kg/m²; current 20.3kg/m²), SBP (12 months ago 120mmHg; 9 months ago 120mmHg; 6 months ago 120mmHg; 3 months ago 120mmHg; current 111mmHg), DBP (12 months ago 68mmHg; 9 months ago 66mmHg; 6 months ago 67mmHg; 3 months ago 66mmHg; current 64mmHg)
- Liver function: ALT (12 months ago 20U/L; 9 months ago 14U/L; 6 months ago 12U/L; 3 months ago 12U/L; current 16U/L), AST (12 months ago 18U/L; 9 months ago 19U/L; 6 months ago 19U/L; 3 months ago 13U/L; current 15U/L), Total Bilirubin (12 months ago 0.4mg/dL; 9 months ago 0.4mg/dL; 6 months ago 0.4mg/dL; 3 months ago 0.4mg/dL; current 0.4mg/dL)
- Kidney: eGFR (12 months ago 28mL/min/1.73m²; 9 months ago 27mL/min/1.73m²; 6 months ago 22mL/min/1.73m²; 3 months ago 24mL/min/1.73m²; current 23mL/min/1.73m²), UACR (12 months ago 382mg/g; 9 months ago 527mg/g; 6 months ago 409mg/g; 3 months ago 609mg/g; current 424mg/g)
- Lipids: LDL (12 months ago 98mg/dL; 9 months ago 98mg/dL; 6 months ago 102mg/dL; 3 months ago 100mg/dL; current 99mg/dL); HDL (12 months ago 38mg/dL; 9 months ago 38mg/dL; 6 months ago 41mg/dL; 3 months ago 41mg/dL; current 39mg/dL); Triglycerides (12 months ago 172mg/dL; 9 months ago 171mg/dL; 6 months ago 172mg/dL; 3 months ago 151mg/dL; current 163mg/dL)
- Hemoglobin: Hemoglobin (12 months ago 10.0g/dL; 9 months ago 10.0g/dL; 6 months ago 10.2g/dL; 3 months ago 9.4g/dL; current 9.6g/dL)
- Additional tests: urine ketone Negative (Current); fasting C-peptide 0.94 ng/mL (Current)

### Individualized treatment decision factors
Current adherence and self-management: Excellent adherence (estimated ≥90%); SMBG 4-5 times/week; diet/exercise well controlled.
Financial context: Patient has comprehensive insurance with minimal out-of-pocket burden and reports no financial barriers to clinically indicated therapies (budget ≤ KRW 1,000,000/month).
